ddls:
CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "parent_category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent category to support hierarchical categories */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the category is currently active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_categories_parent_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_category_id") REFERENCES categories ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ingredients" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each ingredient */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the ingredient */,
  "is_allergen" INTEGER /* Indicates if the ingredient is an allergen */,
  "supplier_id" INTEGER /* ID of the supplier for the ingredient */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the ingredient is currently active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ingredients_supplier_id FOREIGN KEY ("supplier_id") REFERENCES suppliers ("supplier_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "menu_item_ingredients" (
  "menu_item_id" INTEGER /* ID of the menu item */,
  "ingredient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the ingredient used in the menu item */,
  "quantity" REAL /* Quantity of the ingredient used in the menu item */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the ingredient (e.g., grams, liters) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("menu_item_id", "ingredient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user in the restaurant (e.g., manager, chef) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "orders" (
  "order_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each order */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who placed the order */,
  "menu_item_id" INTEGER /* ID of the menu item ordered */,
  "order_date" TEXT /* Date when the order was placed */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the menu item ordered */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the order (e.g., pending, completed, canceled) */,
  "total_price" REAL /* Total price of the order */,
  "delivery_address" TEXT /* Address for delivery (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_orders_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "inventory" (
  "item_id" INTEGER /* ID of the item in the inventory (menu item or ingredient) */,
  "ingredient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the ingredient related to the inventory */,
  "quantity_available" INTEGER /* Quantity available in stock */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the inventory was last updated */,
  "threshold" INTEGER /* Minimum quantity threshold to trigger a reorder */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("item_id", "ingredient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "suppliers" (
  "supplier_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each supplier */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the supplier */,
  "contact_name" TEXT /* Name of the contact person at the supplier */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the supplier */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the supplier */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the supplier */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the supplier is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("supplier_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the location */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the location is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "menu_item_locations" (
  "menu_item_id" INTEGER /* ID of the menu item */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location */,
  "availability" INTEGER /* Indicates if the menu item is available at the location */,
  "price" REAL /* Price of the menu item at the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("menu_item_id", "location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "order_status_history" (
  "order_id" INTEGER /* ID of the order */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the order */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date when the status was updated */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who updated the status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_id", "updated_at"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_order_status_history_updated_by FOREIGN KEY ("updated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
